pattern: Every character's final leftover count only depends on how many times it occurs total, not on where -- a majority-count character shrinks to one survivor if its count is odd and two if it's even.
hints: Removing the closest left and right occurrence of a repeated character can be done repeatedly as long as at least three of it remain reachable, so think about what's left per character, in isolation from the others.
For a single letter appearing n times, pairs can keep being trimmed from around some middle occurrence until only one (if n is odd) or two (if n is even) remain.
Count each letter's occurrences in the string; the answer is the sum, over letters that appear at all, of 1 if that letter's count is odd and 2 if it's even.

edge_inputs.description: statement example with a mix of odd and even counts
statement example where nothing can be removed
every letter distinct, nothing to remove
one letter repeated an odd number of times
one letter repeated an even number of times

stress.description: random strings over a small alphabet at realistic scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: n scales up to the real constraint
